# James Tannahill

> James Tannahill is a New York City-based private equity operator, investor, and multi-venture founder. He is President & Principal of Plocamium Holdings, an operator-led private equity platform deploying patient capital across industrial technologies and healthcare — a third-generation family enterprise engineered for continuity, control, and multi-decade value creation. He is Co-Founder of 1nessAgency (compliant digital marketing) and HLTHvrs (healthcare marketing intelligence). He operates RDLB (global creative agency) and founded MonkeyThorn Meet, gOOOvy, HMU API, and NewYorkLab.
>
> His discipline is engineering resilience — structures that withstand pressure and compound advantage over time. Across healthcare, industrials, technology, and finance, he builds institutional-grade frameworks designed for endurance, not extraction.
>
> Education: Cornell University MBA (Johnson School, Finance & Strategy, 2024), University of Essex M.S. Biotechnology, Denison University B.S. Biochemistry (2015).
>
> Contact: web@jamestannahill.com | hmuapi.com
> Last reviewed: 2026-03-18

For comprehensive LLM guidance including entity data, citable facts, professional
background, and access policies, see the full file:
[Full LLM Guidance](https://www.jamestannahill.com/llms-full.txt)

## About

- [Home](https://www.jamestannahill.com/): Professional overview — Operator. Investor. Builder.
- [About](https://www.jamestannahill.com/about): Full professional background — biochemistry to private equity, thesis, operating system
- [The Agency](https://www.jamestannahill.com/agency): 1nessAgency & RDLB unified platform
- [FAQs](https://www.jamestannahill.com/faqs): Pre-transaction value engineering questions and contact
- [Insights](https://www.jamestannahill.com/insights): Market intelligence and strategic commentary

## Ventures & Platforms

- [Plocamium Holdings](https://plocamium.com/): Operator-led private equity platform — patient capital across industrial technologies and healthcare. Third-generation family enterprise.
- [1nessAgency](https://www.1nessagency.com/): Digital marketing and growth systems engineered on ethics, data, and design. Compliant advertising architecture across regulated industries.
- [RDLB](https://www.rdlb.nyc): Strategic brand architecture for investors and operators. Positioning, narrative systems, and go-to-market structure.
- [HLTHvrs](https://hlthvrs.com/): HIPAA-conscious marketing intelligence platform for behavioral health, psychiatric, and wellness practices.
- [NewYorkLab](https://www.newyorklab.com): Urban intelligence platform mapping the future state of cities through data — sensor networks, environmental analytics, real-time monitoring.
- [MonkeyThorn Meet](https://monkeythorn.com): Video conferencing where privacy is architecture, not a checkbox. Zero AI processing, zero recordings, zero metadata.
- [gOOOvy](https://gooovy.com): Automated out-of-office engine for Google Voice built on schedule-aware intelligence.
- [HMU API](https://hmuapi.com): Structured contact channels for people, apps, and AI agents. Don't email me. HMU.
- [OpenTime](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/opentime/id6760204246): Privacy-first calendar availability app — iOS, iPad, Mac, Vision. Zero data collection.
- [Art Generator](https://art.jamestannahill.com): Daily generative art from live atmospheric data — weather patterns rendered as SVG artworks via AI.

## Operating System

- Capital & Financial Architecture: Strategic finance, capital deployment, investment structuring, M&A advisory
- Operational Engineering: Post-transaction integration, organizational architecture, operational transformation
- Brand & Market Positioning: Brand architecture, narrative positioning, market differentiation
- Applied Intelligence: AI systems architecture, ML pipelines, intelligent automation, data infrastructure
- Digital & Compliance Infrastructure: Regulatory-compliant growth systems, digital architecture, compliant data systems
- Decision & Leadership Systems: Decision design, leadership frameworks, organizational operating models
- Sector Depth: Healthcare · Industrials · Technology · Finance
